title: T-112 — Outbox worker consumer registered in apps/worker (P0 blocker for every domain event)
prompt:
# T-112 — Outbox worker consumer registered in apps/worker

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §10 (Outbox pattern od MVP), §5 Backend (Outbox dispatcher), §13 Funkcjonalne (Outbox events emitowane dla każdej business mutacji)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register the existing `@monopilot/outbox` `runOnce()` worker (T-008 / `packages/outbox/src/worker.ts`) as a scheduled interval job inside the `apps/worker` registry (T-111). Today every cascade/schema-publish/cohort-advance/audit event written to `outbox_events` accumulates — confirmed by `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §4.3 ("Production caller: MISSING") and Auditor-A Top-10 Gap #5. This task supplies the production caller, exponential-backoff retry, processed-row marking, and a unit test proving the dispatch loop.

## Implementation contract
1. Create `apps/worker/src/jobs/outbox-consumer.ts` that exports `registerOutboxConsumer(registry, opts)` where `opts = { everyMs?: number, batchSize?: number, retry?: { maxAttempts: number; baseDelayMs: number } }`. Defaults: `everyMs=5_000`, `batchSize=100`, `maxAttempts=5`, `baseDelayMs=500`.
2. The handler acquires a PoolClient from the worker's shared pool, calls `runOnce(client, queue, schema)` from `@monopilot/outbox`, then releases. Errors per row must NOT abort the batch — each row that throws is left with `consumed_at` NULL and an `attempts` counter incremented (add column if missing via a new migration `packages/db/migrations/0NNN-outbox-attempts.sql` — verify migration index first by listing `packages/db/migrations/`).
3. Exponential-backoff: if `attempts ≥ maxAttempts`, the row is moved to `outbox_dead_letter` (create table in same migration: same columns as `outbox_events` + `failed_at`, `last_error_text`). Idempotent partial-failure semantics: a row is either consumed, retry-pending, or dead-lettered — never lost.
4. The Queue implementation in production wiring is a stub `LoggingQueue` (logs JSON line) until a real bus lands; tests inject `InMemoryQueue` from `@monopilot/outbox`.
5. Wire registration in `apps/worker/src/index.ts`: after JobRegistry is constructed, call `registerOutboxConsumer(registry, { everyMs: env.OUTBOX_INTERVAL_MS })`.
6. Add `apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/outbox-consumer.test.ts`: spin up a real testcontainers Postgres (the repo already uses it per `packages/db/__tests__/*`). Seed one `outbox_events` row + one row that throws + one row exceeding maxAttempts; assert: (i) successful row marked consumed, (ii) throwing row stays pending with attempts=1, (iii) maxAttempts-exceeded row moves to `outbox_dead_letter`.

## Files in scope
- apps/worker/src/jobs/outbox-consumer.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/jobs/__tests__/outbox-consumer.test.ts [create]
- apps/worker/src/index.ts [modify — register the job]
- apps/worker/src/env.ts [modify — add OUTBOX_INTERVAL_MS]
- packages/db/migrations/0NNN-outbox-attempts-and-dlq.sql [create — verify next free NNN]
- packages/db/src/schema/outbox.ts [modify if Drizzle schema exists]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given outbox_events row with payload, when worker tick runs, then row consumed_at is set and registered Queue receives one publish call with normalized event_type.
2. Given handler throws for specific aggregate_id, when tick runs, then attempts incremented to 1, consumed_at remains NULL, next tick re-attempts.
3. Given row attempts ≥ maxAttempts(=5), when tick processes, then row INSERTed into outbox_dead_letter with last_error_text, marked in outbox_events with dead_lettered_at, never re-attempted.
4. Given apps/worker started, when shutdown is requested mid-tick, then in-flight tick drains and pool ends; AND Given new migration ran, when \d outbox_events inspected, then attempts INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 exists; outbox_dead_letter has mirrored RLS using app.current_org_id().
## Test strategy
- RED: write `outbox-consumer.test.ts` asserting ACs 1/2/3/5 (AC4 via shutdown test using `runOnceForTest` from T-111 registry). RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test`.
- GREEN: implement the job, migration, and registration; testcontainers Postgres applies the migration and runs the scenarios.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/outbox test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not call `runOnce()` from a Next.js Route Handler or Server Action — the production caller MUST be `apps/worker` per §10 (no request-path background work).
- Do not skip the `attempts` migration — silently leaving rows pending without attempt-count is the root cause of the accumulation bug audited 2026-05-14.
- Do not bypass RLS on `outbox_dead_letter` — same org_id scoping as parent.
- Do not drop rows on failure — dead-letter, never delete.
- Do not import the consumer from `apps/web` or any module package — it lives in `apps/worker` only.
- Do not change `@monopilot/outbox` runOnce signature — the worker adapts around it.

pipeline_inputs.description: Register @monopilot/outbox runOnce as scheduled interval job in apps/worker, add attempts column + outbox_dead_letter table, exponential backoff retry, partial-batch fault isolation, vitest with testcontainers Postgres.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Worker tick (every 5s default) acquires a pool client, calls runOnce, releases. Per-row failure increments attempts; ≥maxAttempts moves row to outbox_dead_letter with last_error_text. RLS enforced on both tables via org_id = app.current_org_id() (foundation non-spoofable function). Production Queue = LoggingQueue stub until real bus; test injects InMemoryQueue. Single shared pg.Pool from JobRegistry.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
out_of_scope: Real Azure Service Bus / MQTT queue adapter (defer to integration tasks)
acceptance_criteria: Given outbox_events row with payload, when worker tick runs, then row consumed_at is set and registered Queue receives one publish call with normalized event_type.
Given handler throws for specific aggregate_id, when tick runs, then attempts incremented to 1, consumed_at remains NULL, next tick re-attempts.
Given row attempts ≥ maxAttempts(=5), when tick processes, then row INSERTed into outbox_dead_letter with last_error_text, marked in outbox_events with dead_lettered_at, never re-attempted.

Given apps/worker started, when shutdown is requested mid-tick, then in-flight tick drains and pool ends; AND Given new migration ran, when \d outbox_events inspected, then attempts INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 exists; outbox_dead_letter has mirrored RLS using app.current_org_id().
test_strategy: RED: write outbox-consumer.test.ts asserting ACs 1/2/3/5 before code; use testcontainers Postgres and InMemoryQueue from @monopilot/outbox; RED command: pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test.
Run: pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/outbox test.
risk_red_lines: Do not call runOnce from a Next.js Route Handler or Server Action — production caller MUST be apps/worker.
Do not skip the attempts migration — silent pending rows is the audited 2026-05-14 accumulation bug.
Do not bypass RLS on outbox_dead_letter — same org_id scoping using app.current_org_id() function.
Do not drop rows on failure — dead-letter, never delete.
Do not import the consumer from apps/web or any module package — apps/worker only.
Do not change @monopilot/outbox runOnce signature — worker adapts around it.
